---
title: "Internationalisation"
---

`quarto-coursegen` supports i18n strings in generated `.qmd` stubs (labels,
section headings, navigation text, etc.).
The active language is set once in `course.yaml`.

---

## Setting the language

```yaml
course:
  language: "de"    # default: "en"
```

Built-in languages: **`en`** (English) and **`de`** (German).

---

## Resolution order

Language strings are resolved from highest to lowest priority:

1. **Inline `i18n:` overrides** in `course.yaml`
2. **First matching `{lang}.yaml`** found across lang directories
   (local overlay first, built-in fallback)
3. **Built-in English defaults** — always the base

---

## Adding a new language

`quarto-coursegen init` copies `lang/en.yaml` and `lang/de.yaml` into your
course project.
To add a new language, create `lang/{code}.yaml` following the same structure:

```yaml
# lang/fr.yaml
nav:
  home: "Accueil"
  modules: "Modules"
  …
```

Partial files are fine — only the keys present override the English defaults.
Omitted keys fall back to English automatically.

---

## Inline overrides

Override individual strings directly in `course.yaml` without creating a
separate lang file:

```yaml
course:
  language: de
  i18n:
    nav:
      home: Startseite    # overrides the German lang file value
```

Inline overrides have the highest priority — they override both the lang file
and the built-in defaults.

---

## Custom lang directory

Pass a custom directory at the command line:

```bash
quarto-coursegen generate --lang-dir /path/to/my/lang
```

Or configure it in `coursegen.yaml`:

```yaml
lang: lang/
```

When multiple lang directories are configured, the first directory that
contains a matching `{lang}.yaml` wins.
The built-in lang files act as the final fallback.
